Article: A few music questions
[]: A few music questions...             Quote Post by TotemoHarley » Sun Nov 22, 2009 6:02 pm Out of curiosity and slight boredom, I've recently made an account at Nico Video and proceeded to browse through some Evangelion videos. A few of the results that piqued my interest appear to be from a video/pachinko game (?). In particular, there were a few pieces of music I don't quite recall being played during the series but are prominently used in these videos. The first one I have a question about can be found here:[url]http://www.nicovideo.jp/watch/sm7626425[/url] In the aforementioned video, the first piece I'm wondering about begins at about 10:32, after Kaworu's introduction. Also, at 12:12, I believe its a continuation of the same piece, and although it sounds much more familar (though not from Eva), I still can't place a name to it. As a result, I'm not sure if it's an original piece for the game or if it derives from an actual piece. In the next video, which can be located here:[url]http://www.nicovideo.jp/watch/sm7967574[/url] At around 7:31 (and several times later in the video) a version of Handel's Hallelujah is played that appears to have a more feminine sound than the one played in the series (and elsewhere). Also, I've heard a remarkably similar (if not identical) piece in this Sgt.Keroro Eva parody: [url]http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=3pv7pJY1USg[/url] If anyone would be able to further identify either of these pieces, whether they've ever been released on an Eva CD somewhere or any other additional information of their origins, I'd greatly appreciate as these songs are currently repeating themselves in my head. XD;;

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Tue Nov 24, 2009 9:46 am my memory is a bit fuzzy but i do believe the questions about the first vid, the 10:32 and 12:12 are Verdi's Requiem which is available on the Death OST

[]:             Quote Post by TotemoHarley » Tue Nov 24, 2009 7:02 pm Ah, that sounds about right. Thank you

Article: A few music questions
 Verdi's Requiem which is available on the Death OST

[]:             Quote Post by TotemoHarley » Tue Nov 24, 2009 7:02 pm Ah, that sounds about right. Thank you so much for your assistance~! =D
